markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spellslasher](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3895)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Whetstone" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=861" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 137

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _spellslasher_ is made of faceted crystal, its edges sharp and oddly misaligned. When it’s applied to a weapon, it attunes the weapon to magical energies, allowing it to slice through spells as though they were physical threads. While your weapon is under the effect of a _spellslasher_, you gain the Spellslash reaction; you can use this reaction only with the affected weapon, and the _spellslasher’s_ effects end as soon as you use Spellslash.

**Spellslash**  <actions string="Reaction" /> ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Trigger** You are the only target of a spell; **Effect** You swing or shoot your weapon to intercept the magic, attempting to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the spell. Your counteract rank is equal to half your level (rounded up), and your counteract check modifier is equal to your attack modifier with the affected weapon.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spellslasher

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This jagged bit of metal is pitted and worn but wickedly sharp. The DC of the flat check to end [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) dealt by a weapon under the effects of a _toothy knife_ is 17 (or 12 with appropriate assistance). This bleeding still typically ends on its own after 1 minute, as normal.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Valorous Coin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3898)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Whetstone" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=861" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 137

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Valorous coins_ are metal disks emblazoned with two crossed swords, with inscriptions exhorting bravery and optimism. The effects of a _valorous coin_ last for 1 hour. During that time, if you’re reduced below a quarter of your Hit Points while wielding the affected weapon, it empowers you with determination and resolve to finish the fight. You gain temporary Hit Points equal to your level that last for 1 minute, and you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Strikes and damage rolls with the affected weapon for 1 minute. Once this minute ends, so do all effects and the remaining duration of the _valorous coin_, and you’re [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) until you’re healed to your maximum Hit Points.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Valorous Coin

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Windstep Sheath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3901)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Whetstone" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=861" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 137

**Price** 101 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Carved of ash wood and decorated with images of clouds and gusting winds, a _windstep sheath_ is a small icon shaped like a weapon sheath or quiver. This whetstone is favored by duelists who like to take their foe by surprise. When you use the [Quick Draw](/Feats.aspx?ID=4869) feat with a weapon under the effect of a _windstep sheath_, you can also [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) as part of that action, either immediately before or after your Strike.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Windstep Sheath

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Arcane Standard](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3902)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 138

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) If a PC uses an arcane standard’s Magical Weakness ability against a creature which has taken more than one type of energy damage, the player chooses which energy type the creature gains weakness to._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed or held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magical banner has glittery, arcane threads crosswoven into the fabric, causing it to reflect light in little sparkles. While holding an _arcane standard_, you can use the following ability.

**Activate—Magical Weakness** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per turn;   **Effect** The magic of the banner causes energy to linger, tearing away at its target, leaving them vulnerable to more. One creature within the banner’s aura that has taken [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage this turn gains [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 5 to that damage type for 1 round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Arcane Standard
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 138

**Price** 960 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 20"
pfs="Standard"
>
Greater Arcane Standard
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 138

**Price** 69,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The weakness inflicted by Magical Weakness increases to 10.
name: Arcane Standard

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Banner of Creeping Death](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3903)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 138

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed or held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The very fabric of this off-putting magical banner seems to be rotting with a slick, foul texture. Traditionally, these banners were created from the uniforms of fallen enemy troops, but this is considered a cruel and dishonorable practice by many modern nations. While holding a _banner of creeping death_, you can use the following ability.

**Activate—Void’s Embrace** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** A massive wave of void energy floods out from the banner in all directions. All living creatures within the banner’s aura take 1d4+1 void damage (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Lesser Banner of Creeping Death
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 120 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

pfs="Standard"

</title>

</traits>

**Price** 750 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The void damage increases to 2d4+2 and the save DC increases to 26.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 14"
pfs="Standard"
>
Greater Banner of Creeping Death
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 138

**Price** 4,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The void damage increases to 4d4+4 and the save DC increases to 32.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Major Banner of Creeping Death
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Banner of Creeping Death](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3903)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 138

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed or held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The very fabric of this off-putting magical banner seems to be rotting with a slick, foul texture. Traditionally, these banners were created from the uniforms of fallen enemy troops, but this is considered a cruel and dishonorable practice by many modern nations. While holding a _banner of creeping death_, you can use the following ability.

**Activate—Void’s Embrace** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** A massive wave of void energy floods out from the banner in all directions. All living creatures within the banner’s aura take 1d4+1 void damage (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Banner of Piercing Shards](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3904)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 138

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed or held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magical banner has an intricately embroidered pattern of shards and cracks across its surface, almost like a broken mirror. Though it always feels dry to the touch, this banner from a distance gleams red as if slightly stained with the blood of your enemies. While holding a _banner of piercing shards_, you can use the following ability.

**Activate—Shards Seek Wounds** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** Shards of sharpened glass violently shoot out from the magical banner into the newly opened wounds of a nearby enemy. The magical banner deals 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) to any enemy within the banner’s aura that has been dealt damage since the end of your last turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Banner of the Restful](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3905)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 139

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed or held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This peach-colored magical banner offers the promise of a good watch and a comfortable sleep. You and allies within the banner’s aura gain a +1 item bonus to Perception DCs and protection from [severe cold and heat](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Banner of the Rising Star](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3906)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 139

**Price** 60,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed or held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A single pale star shines bravely amid the dark cloth of this magical banner. The star can be seen even in the dead of night. While holding a _banner of the rising star_, you can use the following ability.

**Activate—Rise Up** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** The magical banner lifts your allies from the brink of death. An ally within the banner’s aura with the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition regains 30 Hit Points, does not increase their [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) condition, and can [Stand](/Actions.aspx?ID=2303) as a free action. They become immune to Rise Up for 1 day.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Banner of the Rising Star

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Beacon of the Wilds](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3907)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 139

**Price** 3,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed or held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magical banner lightens your feet and allows you to move adroitly. This banner is sometimes referred to as the marching flag. You and your allies [ignore difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) within the banner’s aura.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Beacon of the Wilds

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blazing Banner](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3908)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed or held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magical banner shimmers in a fiery array of reds, oranges, and yellows. The rampant threads catch light in the wind and give the appearance of a blazing flame. Whenever you or an ally within the banner’s aura critically succeeds with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), the Strike deals an additional 1d4 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Price** 2,000 gp

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dawnfire Beacon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3909)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed or held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The warm, caring light of the sun glows from the center of this magical banner, mirroring the dawn. This magical banner exudes [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in the banner’s aura (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) in an area equal to twice the banner’s aura). This effect is suppressed when you aren’t holding the banner or wielding the weapon it is affixed to.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Dawnfire Beacon
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 140

**Price** 45 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>
Major Dawnfire Beacon
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 140

**Price** 2,600 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This magical banner also grants a +1 status bonus to saving throws against [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) effects to all creatures within the banner’s aura.
name: Dawnfire Beacon

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed or held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The warm, caring light of the sun glows from the center of this magical banner, mirroring the dawn. This magical banner exudes [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in the banner’s aura (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) in an area equal to twice the banner’s aura). This effect is suppressed when you aren’t holding the banner or wielding the weapon it is affixed to.

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magical banner is off-white with a depiction of a stronghold that’s often colored in a striking blue. Those who stand under the banner are prepared to face the weapons of war and defend their keep until the end. You and allies within the banner’s aura gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 5 to damage from siege weapons.

    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hexwise Banner](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3911)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 140

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed or held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Multicolored threads are woven through this magical banner, causing it to appear purple in some light and green in others. The shimmering light offers hope and safety in the face of powerful magic wielders. You and allies within the banner’s aura gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 5 to damage from spells; for spells that apply multiple instances of damage, such as [_force barrage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), this applies only to the first instance of damage.

This magical banner flaps enthusiastically in the breeze, gleaming across the battlefield. While holding a quickened standard, you can use the following ability.

**Activate—Speed Up** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** The banner offers a magical boost of adrenaline. An ally within the banner’s aura becomes [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 round and can use the additional action only to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305).

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magical banner mimics the rich green of summer grass. While holding a _stalwart’s banner_, you can use the following ability.

**Activate—Stand Firm** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** You and allies within your banner’s aura gain 5 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) and a +1 status bonus to your Fortitude DC and Reflex DC against any effect that would move you or knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). These effects last for 1 round.

This magical banner reminds those who view it of the strong bond they have with their comrades on the battlefield. Whenever you or an ally within the banner’s aura uses an action on your turn to prepare to help with [Aid](/Rules.aspx?ID=2552), they can [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) towards an ally as part of that action. They then become immune to this effect for 10 minutes.

As this magical banner sways in the breeze, the horses embroidered across the fabric seem to gallop at unnatural speeds, racing across the field. You and allies that start your turn within the banner’s aura gain a +5-foot status bonus to land Speeds for 1 round. This bonus is doubled while traveling.

This magical banner seems to impossibly be made from many turning gears that encourage viewers to keep perfect time. Whenever you or an ally within the banner’s aura uses the [Delay](/Actions.aspx?ID=2294) or [Ready](/Actions.aspx?ID=2299) action, you or the ally gain 5 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 minute and then become immune to this effect for 10 minutes.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Titan's Standard](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3919)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 141

**Price** 24,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed or held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magical banner stands largest on any battlefield. While holding a _titan’s standard_, you can use the following ability.

**Activate—Titan’s Stature** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** The magical banner causes a rapid surge of growth. A Medium or smaller ally within the banner’s aura becomes Large for 1 round. Its equipment grows with it but returns to its natural size afterwards. While Large, the ally is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1, and its reach increases by 5 feet (or by 10 feet if it started out Tiny).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Titan's Standard

This magical banner stands as a reminder to fight with everything because you’re fighting for everything. While holding a _zealous banner_, you can use the following ability.

**Activate—Forward with Zeal** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** The magical banner offers a magical boost of zeal. <title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Atlas Arcane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3922)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 142

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This well-worn vellum scroll has edges trimmed with golden thread, and it unrolls to reveal a map of the nearby area. The _atlas arcane_ always shows the surrounding area (out to a 36- mile radius centered on the map) with a reasonable level of detail, providing a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks and any skill checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Rules.aspx?ID=2638), provided the checks are related to the location detailed on the map.

**Activate—Situation Report** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You speak a command phrase, and the map reveals the location of all troop movements within the area it maps. This intel is current the moment the phrase is spoken but does not update afterward, and moving the map does not reveal further intel.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Atlas Arcane

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Baton of the Fallen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3923)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 142

**Price** 9,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pale shaft of wood has skulls and the mournful faces of the dead carved into its surface. Cold to the touch, it brings a slight chill to the air and smells of freshly disturbed earth.

**Activate—Gather the Fallen** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You plant the baton into the ground, the soil softly parting to allow it to be solidly seated. Doing so summons a cloud of ghostly spirits in a 30- foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385). All creatures within the cloud become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and all creatures outside the cloud become concealed to creatures within it. The spirits deal 8d6 void damage to each creature who enters or begins their turn in the cloud (DC 35 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude). You are unaffected by the cloud. This effect lasts 1 minute or ends if an adjacent creature spends an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to knock the baton over.

</column>

This drum grants you a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with the instrument. You can communicate basic ideas with whales and other large sea animals by playing music with this instrument.

**Activate—Call from the Depths** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** You drum a song of an ancient creature, calling forth the cries of a great whale in the minds of your foes. All enemies in a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) take 9d10 sonic damage (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). Creatures who fail are also [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.

For the next minute, you can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) to continue the music, granting you and all allies within a 60-foot emanation 5 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 round; this Sustain action gains the auditory and manipulate traits.

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</column>

---

This plain, faintly green bottle full of sand looks deceptively mundane, though it contains a near endless supply of sand. A very slow but steady stream of sand can be poured from the bottle. Up to 1 pound of sand can be produced a day this way.

**Activate—Grit Geyser** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You aim the bottle and unleash a blast of sand. Scouring grit is released in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386), dealing 4d4 slashing damage to all creatures in the area (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).

**Activate—Sandstorm** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You dump out the bottle, creating a swirling sandstorm around you. A 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) is filled with blowing sand that obscures vision. This has the effects of mist. The air within the sandstorm is unbreathable; creatures in the area must [hold their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439). Creatures entering or starting their turn in the sandstorm take 2d4 slashing damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Creatures with the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) trait or that are primarily made of liquid take double damage. This sandstorm lasts 10 minutes or until the bottle is corked with an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action, whichever comes first.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bottle of Infinite Dust

The switch is treated as a simple melee weapon for the purpose of proficiency. This attack deals no damage. **Critical Success** You and your allies gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls against the target for 1 minute. If you reduce the target to 0 Hit Points during this time, you gain [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to twice the target’s level for 1 round.
**Success** As critical success, except you gain temporary Hit Points equal to the target’s level.
**Failure** You and your allies take a –1 status penalty to attack rolls against the target for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** You and your allies take a –2 status penalty to attack rolls against the target for 1 round.

---

This large feather is garishly colored in reds, oranges, and yellows, but on closer inspection, it’s simply a particularly large chicken feather that’s been dyed. Carrying the feather provides a boost to your self-confidence, but it might lead you into dangerous situations.

**Activate—Overconfident Facade** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For 1 hour, the feather grants you a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) and [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392), but a –1 item penalty on [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) and [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks, as your inflated confidence leads you to attempt things you simply cannot do.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Feather of the Unfounded Bravado

You and all allies in a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws for 1 round.

They also gain a +1 status bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) checks to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) and automatically succeed when they Command an Animal they’re mounted on to take a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action (such as [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305)).

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 144 **Price** 8,500 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- Crafted by [archons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=365) for mortal use, this trumpet is made of luminous gold and ivory, giving off a soft glow that warms the soul. This trumpet grants you a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with the instrument. **Activate—Archon's Note** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You blast a note on the horn so clear and pure that its grandeur stuns your enemies and inspires your allies. Allies in the area gain a +1 status bonus to attack rolls and saving throws for 1 round. Enemies within a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 35 Fortitude saving throw. They’re then temporarily immune for 1 day. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 and off-guard for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stunned 2 and off-guard for 1 round.
name: Horn of the Archon

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 144 **Price** 7,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- This blasphemous idol has the vague outline of a recumbent humanoid, but the more detail one perceives, the more its alien nature is revealed. The mere presence of the effigy causes disturbing dreams, and anyone who sleeps within 50 feet of the item must succeed at a DC 30 Will save or awaken [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73). **Activate—Whispers of Leng** 10 minutes ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You whisper a name and whatever details you recall about that person to the effigy, telling it everything you can about your target. The next time the target sleeps, they are subject to a [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615) spell (DC 30). This continues every time the target sleeps until they die, you whisper a new target to the effigy, or the target gets a critical success on their save against the _nightmare_. **Activate—Smothering Lassitude** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You brandish the effigy aloft, exposing all who see it to its bizarre visage. You and all creatures within a 120-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 34 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 until the start of your next turn.
**Failure** The creature is stupefied 1 and [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 until the start of your next turn.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stupefied 2 and stunned 1 until the start of your next turn.
name: Horrific Effigy

[Hungry Lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3940)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 145

**Price** 1,900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This lantern is made of weathered tin and is cold and moist to the touch. The interior of the lantern, where a wick or candle would normally be placed, is filled with thick, black smoke, and there’s no way to open the lantern.

**Activate—Consuming Darkness** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** Pure, impenetrable darkness flows out of the lantern like smoke and simply eats the light. A 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) centered on the lantern is plunged into darkness for 1 minute. This darkness functions as a 4th-rank [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) spell. When the darkness is created, it deals 6d8 spirit damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save) to all creatures within the area. Any creature reduced to 0 Hit Points from this damage is destroyed entirely, leaving behind only a shadow that will slowly fade over the course of a year.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hungry Lantern

This short, thick, stick-like object is crafted of wood and steel. Precious metals decorate the grip, and fine filigree marks the caps on each end. A _marshal’s baton_ grants you a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) and [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks against troops, individual soldiers, and military leaders.

**Activate—Stentorian Order** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You issue a command in a booming voice while gesturing with the _marshal’s baton_ and cast a [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470) spell (DC 18). This spell affects [troops](/Rules.aspx?ID=3364) and [swarms](/Traits.aspx?ID=707) as if they were a single creature.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pocket Watch of Stethelos](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3942)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 146

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This brass pocket watch has an unusually complex winding mechanism, and the name inscribed on the back, as well as the numbers on the face, are written in a language no one can identify. Repeated use of the pocket watch attracts the attention of terrible creatures of the [Dimension of Time](/Planes.aspx?ID=46).

**Activate—Steal a Second** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You manipulate time around an ally within 30 feet, allowing them to perceive time differently for a moment. The target is [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 round and can use the action only to [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305).

**Activate—Step Between the Ticks** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 3 actions, each of which must be immediately used to [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), [Stand](/Actions.aspx?ID=2303), [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305). If you have an appropriate Speed, you can add [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310), [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) to this list. While you take these actions, time pauses. All other creatures are completely unaware of your actions, can’t speak, and can’t use any actions that would be triggered by your movements. While you’re taking these actions, you can’t take any other actions, including any that would be triggered by the move actions. Once the actions are complete, time starts again, and to onlookers, you seem to have suddenly teleported across the distance you traveled.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pocket Watch of Stethelos

**Activate—Sow a Tooth** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You cast forth a tooth from the bag, and where it lands, a skeleton springs up from the ground. This has the effect of [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631), except the minion appears as a visible skeleton.

**Activate—Fist Full of Fangs** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You draw a whole handful of teeth and cast them to the ground, casting [_rouse skeletons_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2019) as a 5th-rank spell (DC 30).

This satchel is made of finely worked leather and stitched with golden thread. A complex pattern of trees, leaves, and vines covers its surface. The satchel always contains a bulk of seeds, and drawing one from the bag results in a random tree or crop plant seed.

**Activate—Seed of Saftey** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You draw a seed and cast it into a space within 30 feet. The satchel casts [_protector tree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2015) as a 2nd-rank spell.

**Activate—Seed of Sustenance** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You draw a seed and cast it into a space within 30 feet. A small tree sprouts within 10 minutes, producing 5 fruits. A creature who eats the fruit with an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action regains 1d6+2 Hit Points and receives as much nourishment as one meal for a typical human. After an hour, the tree and all its fruits wither away.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Satchel of Numberless Seeds

**Failure** The target is frightened 1 and is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is frightened 2 and off-guard for 2 rounds.

At the GM’s discretion, creatures with major serpentine features, such as [serpentfolk](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=468), are also subjected to this effect. You and all allies within a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) gain the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) condition until the end of your next turn and can use the extra action each round for only [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) and [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) actions. **Activate—Larghissimo** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You play the snare with a lassitude that drains the speed from your foes. Enemies within a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 34 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is slowed 2 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is slowed 2 and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round.

This stick is a decorative wooden baton with a metal cap on one end and a stylized handle in the shape of a horse or other martial beast on the other. Mundane swagger sticks are carried by officers in armies all across Golarion. This one is made of particularly fine wood, with an aged silver cap and handle, and small garnets for the creature’s eyes.

**Activate—Swagger** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You dramatically swing, twirl, or otherwise brandish the _swagger stick_ to direct your troops. All allied creatures within 30 feet who can see your display gain +1 status bonus to attack rolls, Fortitude saves, and Will saves against mental effects for 1 round.

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Abidance Blinders](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3953)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 148

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear (animal) and worn headwear (you)

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While many skilled cavalry units have their own beloved mounts, troops often need to make do with whatever animals are available for mounted combat. These blinders, made of dark brown leather, nearly cover an animal’s eyes and prevent it from seeing the dangers around them. It takes 1 minute to affix the blinders on an animal, whose attitude toward you must be indifferent or better. These blinders can be used only on a domesticated animal that doesn’t have combat training or the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) trait. Animals with these blinders on do not suffer from the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition due to being in combat, nor do they automatically flee combat, as would be normal for an [animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=2219) without combat training. Unless mounted by a rider with the paired headband, which is braided from the same dark brown leather, the animal’s speed is reduced to 10 feet.

**Activate—It Can’t Hurt You** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An animal within 30 feet wearing the paired blinders attempts a saving throw against a [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effect but hasn’t rolled yet;  **Effect** You grant a +1 status bonus to its saving throw against the triggering effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Abidance Blinders

**Activate—Staunch Bleeding** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The stain on the _bloodstained waistcoat_ gets slightly larger as you bleed in lieu of an ally. The waistcoat ends a persistent bleed condition for one ally within 30 feet, but you gain that condition with the same parameters.

</row>

</column>

---

Perhaps you had no other choice than to steal the boots off a fallen soldier. Yours may have been worn out, full of holes, or even coming apart. Did you take them from a fallen ally or adversary? Everyone looks the same after war has its way. Nevertheless, the guilt you feel weighs you down. You gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws and DCs against forced movement.

**Activate—One of You** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You shuffle your boots, which still stink of the dead, causing one [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature of your choice to think that you too are undead. The target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against the next melee attack you attempt against it before the end of your current turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Boots of the Dead

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Comandant's Scabbard](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3961)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 149

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Only the leader of an army could wear this diamond and ruby-encrusted scabbard that somehow always remains shiny no matter how terrible the conditions. While wearing the scabbard, you feel exceptionally powerful, and you gain a +3 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks. When you invest the scabbard, you either increase your Strength modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value. 

The _commandant’s scabbard_ holds one melee weapon of 1 Bulk or lighter. Whenever a weapon is drawn from the scabbard, it gains a +4 status bonus to damage for 1 round.

**Activate—Commanding Draw** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** You have a weapon sheathed in your _commandant’s scabbard_; **Effect** You [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to draw your weapon from your scabbard and [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with it. On that Strike, you can roll twice and take the better result.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Comandant's Scabbard

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Endless Quiver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3962)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 149

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Elite archers can go through countless arrows over the course of a battle. Smart ones carry an _endless quiver_ to ensure they never run out. This quiver holds 40 mundane arrows and regenerates 10 per hour. Once an arrow is removed from the _endless quiver_, it dissipates after 1 minute.

**Activate—Convert Arrows** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You tap the quiver, and the arrows inside transform into [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920). They revert to wood after 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Endless Quiver
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 149

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Greater Endless Quiver
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 149

**Price** 19,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The quiver holds 100 arrows at once. You can use Convert Arrows to also transform your arrows into [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) or [dawnsilver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2917).
name: Endless Quiver

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

First developed by blacksmiths to move burning hot workpieces around, _fireproof gloves_ then became popular with soldiers responsible for disabling bombs and magical traps. The thick tan gloves come up over the arm. When wearing these gloves, you gain fire [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 5.

**Activate—Release Heat** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You have a free hand; **Effect** You take the heat that’s built up in your gloves and discharge it onto an enemy. You deal 6d8 fire damage to one creature within reach (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gas Mask of Clean Air](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3964)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 149

**Price** 85 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This black canvas mask covers your mouth and nose, with thick tubes coming from the sides. While wearing this mask, you gain a +1 item bonus to saves against [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=634) [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), inhaled diseases, and [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664) effects.

**Activate—Breathe Clean** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your mask springs to life, pumping clean air into your nose and mouth. For 1 round, you are [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to inhaled poisons, inhaled diseases, and olfactory effects. If you have ongoing effects due to such an effect from before activating the mask, those effects continue as normal. If the air around you is unbreathable, you are underwater, or you are in a vacuum, you can breathe normally.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gas Mask of Clean Air

    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Golden Greaves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3965)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 150

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These shiny greaves made of splinted metal coated with gold protect the shins and help you stand your ground in the heat of battle. While wearing the greaves, you gain a +1 item bonus to your Fortitude DC against [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=2364) effects and to your Reflex DC against effects that would knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

**Activate—Make Them Fall** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An enemy fails to [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) you;  **Effect** Your _golden greaves_ glow with a strange light, and you move your legs in just the right way to completely throw off your opponent. Your opponent instead critically fails on the triggering check.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Golden Greaves

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

---

A good pair of gloves is a critical item for any soldier, particularly if you plan to get up close to your enemy. When you wear these black leather gloves with fine silver stitching, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), and [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379).

**Activate—Sticky Grip** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You have an enemy g[rabbed ](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77)or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90); **Effect** Your gloves help you squeeze even more tightly, keeping your opponent from moving freely. The enemy you have grabbed or restrained is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Grippy Gloves

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Instrument Harness](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3967)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 150

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Many armies recruit musicians among their number, who might keep time for marching with a drum, play out commands with a bugle, or intimidate foes with the bagpipes. Regardless of instrument, these musicians are still soldiers and need to be able to fight as well as play at the drop of a hat. The _instrument harness_ is made from white leather decorated with gold musical symbols. You can attach up to 3 Bulk of musical instruments to the harness. If you drop an attached instrument, it remains safely at your side rather than dropped to the ground.

**Activate—Ready to Play** <actions string="Free Action" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** There is an instrument attached to the _instrument harness_ and you have enough hands free to hold it; **Effect** Your harness ripples, pulling the required instrument into your hands. You [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to draw the required instrument, but this [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) action doesn’t trigger reactions.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Instrument Harness

>
[Killer's Belt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3968)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 150

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Small blood-red rubies decorate this black leather belt, which is a fashion accessory for only the most bloodthirsty soldiers. When you wear this belt, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks.

**Activate—Bleeding Rubies** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You have a free hand and your last action was to deal damage to an enemy with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) or spell attack roll; **Effect** You pull a ruby off your belt and crush it into dust. As this dust reaches the enemy you just harmed, it embeds into the skin, causing them to bleed. The target takes 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The ruby reappears on the belt after 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 4"

>
Killer’s Belt
</title>

</title>

</title>

<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 150

**Price** 240 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You wear a brightly colored sash around your waist as a symbol of your new position. You’re bound to make mistakes in this role, but if you can learn from them, perhaps you’ll live long enough to become an officer. If you trigger a reaction from an enemy or a hazard, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws you attempt as a result of that reaction and a +1 circumstance bonus to your AC against attacks made during that reaction.

**Activate—Heads Up!** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** One of your allies triggers a reaction from an enemy or a hazard;  **Effect** You share your hard-earned experience with your ally, giving them a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws they attempt as a result of that reaction and a +1 circumstance bonus to their AC against attacks made during that reaction.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lieutenant's Sash

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Locket of Love Left Behind](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3970)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 150

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Replacing the casing of a _locket of love left behind_ takes 30 days of Downtime._

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

There’s a tiny picture of a beloved partner, child, or even place nestled inside this small gold locket with a heart on the front. The locket reminds you that no matter how terrible the ravages of war are, you have something very important to live for. While wearing the necklace, you gain a +5-foot status bonus to your Speeds when you have the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) condition. If you are dying, the DC of [recovery checks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2326) is reduced by 1.

**Activate—True Love’s Power** <actions string="Reaction" /> ;   **Trigger** You would die due to a [death effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331) rather than the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition;  **Effect** Your love pulls you back from the brink of death and the locket cracks. You avoid dying and remain at 1 Hit Point. You cannot use the ability again until you replace the casing of the locket, which typically takes around 1 month and costs 600 gp.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Locket of Love Left Behind

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magical Medals](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3971)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Military leaders or heads of state award these special medals to commend exemplary performance by their top soldiers. They’re typically worn on a special strip of fabric near the lapel, but soldiers from different countries sometimes wear them in other places. No matter how many _magical medals_ you have, they collectively count as one invested item.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of Gorilla’s Might
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This steel medal resembles the head and upper body of a [gorilla](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2827). It’s typically given in recognition of physical prowess, and it grants you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377), and [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378).

**Activate—Gorila's Second Leap** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would fail but not critically fail an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378);  **Effect** The gorilla on the medal beats its chest, giving you another chance. You reroll the failed check.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of Griffon’s Heart
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This copper medal features a [griffon’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3034) face, wings, and talons in profile. It is given in recognition of remarkable bravery, and it grants you a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects. While wearing this pendant, you can also cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as an [innate](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) cantrip.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of the Wolf Pack
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

There are three [wolf’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3241) heads engraved on this pewter medal, typically awarded to squads who demonstrate exceptional teamwork. While wearing the _medal of the wolf pack_, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to damage rolls against enemies you are [flanking](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of Unicorn’s Purity
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 1,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The profile of the [unicorn](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=504) on this silver medal has a horn that extends slightly beyond the circular border. This medal is awarded in recognition of true righteousness and exemplary service to a cause.

**Activate—Unicorn’s Grace** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would regain Hit Points from a [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) effect;  **Effect** You regain an additional 4d6 Hit Points.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of Phoenix’s Fire
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 9,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The [phoenix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3137) on this gold medal is proud and fierce, and the medal features a border shaped like a flame. This medal is considered the highest honor in many countries and is awarded to those soldiers who have truly exemplified the ethics of their country through unbelievable service.

**Activate—Phoenix’s Sacrifice** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Your [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition increases;  **Effect** The phoenix bursts into flames. You lose the dying condition and regain 1 Hit Point. Your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) value does not increase. You can use this action while [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95).
name: Magical Medals

right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of the Wolf Pack
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

There are three [wolf’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3241) heads engraved on this pewter medal, typically awarded to squads who demonstrate exceptional teamwork. While wearing the _medal of the wolf pack_, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to damage rolls against enemies you are [flanking](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of Unicorn’s Purity
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 1,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The profile of the [unicorn](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=504) on this silver medal has a horn that extends slightly beyond the circular border. This medal is awarded in recognition of true righteousness and exemplary service to a cause.

**Activate—Unicorn’s Grace** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would regain Hit Points from a [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) effect;  **Effect** You regain an additional 4d6 Hit Points.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of Phoenix’s Fire
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 9,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The [phoenix](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3137) on this gold medal is proud and fierce, and the medal features a border shaped like a flame. This medal is considered the highest honor in many countries and is awarded to those soldiers who have truly exemplified the ethics of their country through unbelievable service.

**Activate—Phoenix’s Sacrifice** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Your [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition increases;  **Effect** The phoenix bursts into flames. You lose the dying condition and regain 1 Hit Point. Your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) value does not increase. You can use this action while [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95).
name: Medal of the Wolf Pack

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Magical Medals](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3971)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Military leaders or heads of state award these special medals to commend exemplary performance by their top soldiers. They’re typically worn on a special strip of fabric near the lapel, but soldiers from different countries sometimes wear them in other places. No matter how many _magical medals_ you have, they collectively count as one invested item.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of Gorilla’s Might
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This steel medal resembles the head and upper body of a [gorilla](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2827). It’s typically given in recognition of physical prowess, and it grants you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374), [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375), [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377), and [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378).

**Activate—Gorila's Second Leap** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would fail but not critically fail an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378);  **Effect** The gorilla on the medal beats its chest, giving you another chance. You reroll the failed check.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medal of Griffon’s Heart
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This copper medal features a [griffon’s](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3034) face, wings, and talons in profile. It is given in recognition of remarkable bravery, and it grants you a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects. While wearing this pendant, you can also cast [_forbidding ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1535) as an [innate](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) cantrip.

</traits>

---

While wearing the _medal of the wolf pack_, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to damage rolls against enemies you are [flanking](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Medic's Armband](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3972)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 151

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You wear this white armband with a bright blue symbol identifying you as a combat medic. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to change the attitudes of [diseased](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [poisoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), and [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) creatures.

**Activate—Do No Harm** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An enemy within 30 feet targets you with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) or a spell that deals damage;  **Effect** Your armband glows, showing that you’re here as a medic and not as a combatant. Both you and the triggering enemy take a –4 status penalty to damage rolls until the end of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Medic’s Armband
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 152

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Greater Medic’s Armband
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 152

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) checks to [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396) and [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=2399), and the status penalty to damage rolls from Do No Harm increases to –8.
name: Medic's Armband

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Motion-Seeking Lenses](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3973)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 152

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you wear these green-tinted glasses, all the movement in the surrounding area seems to jump out at you. You gain a +1 item bonus to your [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) DC against [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405), and anyone attempting to Sneak doesn’t benefit from the circumstance bonus from [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) against your Perception DC.

**Activate—Find the Hidden** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You twist the lenses of your glasses as you look for someone hidden. You [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) with a +1 item bonus. If you find a [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) creature or object, you can [Point Out](/Actions.aspx?ID=2315) as a free action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Motion-Seeking Lenses

**Activate—Alert Superior Officer** <actions string="Free Action" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You gain the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition;  **Effect** The pendent alerts all other allies within 500 feet who are also wearing a _name pendant_.

Troops fighting in locales filled with insects wear enchanted gauzy nets over their heads, draped from wide flat hats, to protect against swarms of stinging or biting creatures. While wearing _protective netting_, if you would be exposed to [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) or injury [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) from an attack, attempt a DC 17 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333). On a success, you are not exposed.

**Activate—Flutter Net** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A swarm enters your space;  **Effect** Your _protective netting_ flutters rapidly, keeping the swarm away. You gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws against effects originating from swarms for 1 minute.

You can breathe underwater for 1 minute.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Trusty Helmet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3983)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 153

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You keep yourself protected from incoming projectiles with this sturdy steel helmet, painted brown.

**Activate—Block Manipulation** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You gain the [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) condition;  **Effect** Your _trusty helmet_ protects not only your head but your mind. The value of your stupefied condition is decreased by 1.

**Activate—Hunker Down** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You hunker down, protecting your head using your helmet. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your AC against ranged attacks.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Trusty Helmet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Underbrush Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3984)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 153

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hooded cloak is lined with rough foliage, vines, and bark that never wilts or rots away. When wearing this cloak, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks when in heavily forested areas.

**Activate—One with the Woods** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For the next minute, you [ignore any difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) caused by plants and fungi, such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Underbrush Cloak

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Warming Parka](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3985)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 153

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garmet

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Only a fool would march an army into a freezing climate without adequate protection. This thick parka with a hood protects you from even the harshest of conditions. You negate the damage from [severe environmental cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820), reduce the damage from extreme cold to that of severe cold, and reduce the damage from incredible cold to extreme cold.

**Activate—Extra Warming** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You draw the hood of your warming parka closed to fend off the cold as much as possible. For the next minute, you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 3 to [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) damage, but also take a –2 item penalty to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) checks. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Warming Parka

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Watcher's Armband](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3986)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Detection" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=43" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 153

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armband

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Soldiers who wear this burgundy armband serve as law enforcement within the ranks of a nation’s military, seeking out those who would commit crimes while in uniform. While wearing the armband, you gain a +2 item bonus to your Perception DC against [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) to you. In addition, you can cast [ring of truth](/Equipment.aspx?ID=607) once per day as an [innate](/Rules.aspx?ID=2901) 3rd-rank [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) spell.

**Activate—Find the Plant** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Sometimes people aren’t in control of their minds. You activate your armband, which, for the next minute, suddenly glows red if anyone within 30 feet of you is under the effect of a [magical ](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect that is controlling their mind or body (such as [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501)).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Watcher's Armband

---

This leather bandolier holds up to three magazines for repeating weapons in leather pockets that pop open with the quick flick of a thumb. You can replace a magazine in a repeating weapon with a magazine from a worn bandolier faster, reducing the number of [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions required by 1. You can wear only one repeater bandolier at a time.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Repeater bandolier

    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[War Saddle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3988)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 119

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Each war saddle is specifically fitted to a mount’s body type and has numerous straps that can secure you on your mount. You remain mounted even if you fall [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) until either you or someone else uses an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to unfasten the straps on the saddle. A creature or effect can separate you from your mount by pulling so hard it tears the straps, but to do so, the effect’s DC, attack roll, or skill check must exceed 20.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: War Saddle
