</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=709" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #216: The Acropolis Pyre](/Sources.aspx?ID=271) pg. 81

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** You are about to roll an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) or [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check, and you are trained or better in the skill; **Requirements** You can perceive at least 12 allies or friendly bystanders encouraging you.</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elegant crown of laurels can be worn on the head, atop a helmet, or even wrapped around the neck as a torc, signaling the wearer's athletic achievements in a past competition. When you Activate the laurels, you draw strength from your adoring crowd and gain a status bonus to the triggering check. If you critically succeed at the check, you gain temporary Hit Points equal to the item's level that last for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</row>

</column>

---

This elegant crown of laurels can be worn on the head, atop a helmet, or even wrapped around the neck as a torc, signaling the wearer's athletic achievements in a past competition. When you Activate the laurels, you draw strength from your adoring crowd and gain a status bonus to the triggering check. If you critically succeed at the check, you gain temporary Hit Points equal to the item's level that last for 1 minute.

[Gift of Olives](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3991)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Plant" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=668" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #216: The Acropolis Pyre](/Sources.aspx?ID=271) pg. 81

**Price** 48 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This expertly trimmed olive branch remains lush and vibrant, even months after being ritually harvested. With a little soil and patience, it can quickly grow into a sheltering tree that later bestows the user with a new gift of olives.

**Activate—Sew the Cutting** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [manipulate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2229), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668))   **Requirements** You are outside and standing atop an unoccupied 5-foot square with some soil; **Effect** You drive one end of the cutting into the soil. The cutting immediately grows into an olive tree whose boughs shelter a 15-foot-diameter area. Creatures in the sheltered area gain resistance 3 to any [environmental damage](/Rules.aspx?ID=2769), gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws against [environmental](/Traits.aspx?ID=591) hazards, and gain a +1 status bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368). After 10 minutes, the tree sprouts 8 olives, each of which can be eaten as an action to restore 1 Hit Point and nourish the eater as if they had consumed a full meal. After 8 hours, the olives lose their potency, and the _gift of olives_ cutting detaches from the tree and falls harmlessly to the ground. The olive tree loses its protective abilities and becomes a normal, non-magical olive tree that survives so long as it's growing in a suitable location.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gift of Olives

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />

This spine and skull of a slain [hydra](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3064) has been picked clean by a carrion bird and crafted into this _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ club](/Weapons.aspx?ID=357)_. In addition to its normal traits, the _hydra head club_ also has the [modular P](/Traits.aspx?ID=807) trait, as it can be manipulated to cause the jaws to spring open. Even in death, its head twitches independently. The creature’s famed regenerative powers are still present, despite the creature’s death; if damaged, the _hydra head club_ repairs itself at the rate of 1 Hit Point per minute, unless it has been completed destroyed.

**Activate—Reactive Snap** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You attempt a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) as a Reaction;  **Effect** The _hydra head club_ snaps with a momentary burst of its former glory. Your Strike attempt deals an additional 1d6 damage. If your Strike misses, the activation does not count against the _hydra head club's_ activation frequency.

**Craft Requirements** You must supply a skull and spine of a hydra when crafting this weapon.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hydra Head Club

The imbued magic allows the stinger to slowly renew a small bit of its venom supply. The wielder can gather this and deliver it during a Strike. The victim is made to see prophetic flashes of their future as it courses through their system.

**Activate—Apiprophecy Sting** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The protector's final gift secrets apiprophecy venom, which coats the blade and affects the next creature successfully struck with the blade within the next minute.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Vortex Pouch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3996)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #217: Death Sails a Wine-Dark Sea
](/Sources.aspx?ID=273) pg. 78

**Price** 240 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When empty, the _vortex pouch’s_ net seems too loose to hold much. However, when used to catch the wind, it stretches to hold the air. With nowhere for the wind to go, the air inside it swirls violently. If the caught wind includes a storm, clouds, [fog](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=4), or similar effect, it’s visible among the whirlwind.

**Activate—Catch Wind** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** The _vortex pouch_ is empty; **Effect** You gather the nearby air and wind within the _vortex pouch_. Creatures within a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save or be moved 5 feet closer toward you as the air rushes into the pouch. Targets that critically fail the save are moved 10 feet. You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the activation once per round, up to 1 minute, to continue Catching Wind. If you spend a [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) when Catching Wind, the vortex pouch gathers air in a 10-mile radius; though the vortex is only strong enough to forcibly suck in creatures within the original 20-foot emanation, weaker creatures with the air trait and creatures associated with the sky in the 10-mile radius will know your general direction and feel a sense of unease, granting you a +2 status bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks against those creatures for 1 week. When you end the activation, the vortex pouch becomes 1 Bulk and you can empty it using Release Wind.

**Activate—Release Wind** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** The _vortex pouch_ contains air or wind; **Effect** Wind rushes out of the _vortex pouch_ in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). All creatures in the cone take 8d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). Creatures that fail their saving throw are pushed 5 feet away from you; creatures that critically fail are pushed 10 feet. If the caught wind included weather that might deal a different type of damage, Release Wind deals this damage instead (for instance, dealing [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) damage if it caught a thundercloud or [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) damage if it caught a [blizzard](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=1)).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vortex Pouch

>
[Preordained Spear](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3998)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 69

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=365)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=365)_ seems to know when its wielder is about to die. Its wooden body bears countless carvings, leaving it thinner than most spears. While you are [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), a preordained spear glows with a faint red light that gives you a haunted look and sheds dim light within 5 feet.

**Activate—Delay Doom** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** Your name is not on the spear, and you have the [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) condition; **Effect** Your name appears on the shaft of the _preordained spear_. Your doomed value decreases by 1, and you regain 1 [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323). Your name fades from the spear the next time you make your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Preordained Spear

>
[Shield of Snakes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3999)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 69

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _shield of snakes_ (Hardness 10, HP 90, BT 45) is built to resemble a withering tangle of snakes. These serpents slither and hiss whenever you [Raise the Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316).

**Activate—Snap Up** <actions string="Reaction" /> ;   **Trigger** You roll initiative;  **Effect** The _shield of snakes_ moves into position, allowing you to [Raise the Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316).

**Activate—Snakebite** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540))    **Effect** You thrust one of the snake heads at a target, attempting a [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=397) [ Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306). If you spend 1 [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323), you attempt this Strike at [mythic proficiency](/Rules.aspx?ID=3322). On a hit, you inflict damage normally, and then the snakes poison the target, inflicting an additional 2d6 poison damage (DC 27 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shield of Snakes

    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Worldscale Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4000)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 69

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

It’s believed that four _worldscale shields_ (Hardness 9, HP 60, BT 30) exist, each made from the scale of one of the four worldscale serpents rumored to burrow through the heart of the world. When a hero-god went to slay one of these serpents, they found that they first needed to tear a scale from the beast’s body to reveal the vulnerable flesh beneath. These four scales removed became the four _worldscale shields_.

**Activate—Absorb Blow** <actions string="Free Action" /> ;   **Trigger** You would take physical damage (bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing) from an attack and you have your _worldscale shield_ [ Raised](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316);  **Effect** Spend 1 [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323). The _worldscale shield_ completely absorbs the blow, preventing all damage that you would have taken from the attack. The _worldscale shield_ breaks. If it was already [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60), it is [destroyed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=46).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Worldscale Shield

    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Aerekostes (Item)](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4001)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="No Alignment" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Intelligent" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=280" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Perception** +16; precise vision 60 feet, imprecise hearing 60 feet

**Communication** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79)

**Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +18, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +16

**Int** +6, **Wis** +4, **Cha** +3

**Will** +16 

</column>

---        

[_Aerekostes_](/Deities.aspx?ID=699) is a _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [shifting ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2846) [ falcata](/Weapons.aspx?ID=282)_. Though unable to move on their own, _Aerekostes_ can flex, adjust their center of mass, and make other adjustments to adapt to a chosen wielder’s fighting style. For the purposes of proficiency, class abilities, and feats (except those that would increase the weapon’s damage die size), a wielder that _Aerekostes_ approves of can treat _Aerekostes_ as a martial weapon that belongs to their choice of the [axe](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1), [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4), [club](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=5), [knife](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=9), [pick](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=10), [spear](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=14), or [sword](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=15) weapon group. _Aerekostes_ does not actually change form and their [critical specialization](/Rules.aspx?ID=2203) effect does not change. 

As a hero-god, _Aerekostes_ is a [mythic](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) being who starts each session with their own pool of 3 [Mythic Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323). Whenever their wielder would regain 2 or more Mythic Points, _Aerekostes_ regains 1 Mythic Point.

**Activate—Forward Planning** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** _Aerekostes_ studies the ongoing combat and myriad ways an upcoming attack might resolve. On the next attack made using _Aerekostes_ before the end of their next turn, their wielder rolls twice and takes the better result.

**Activate—Spell Reserve** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** _Aerekostes_ channels their [mythic power](/Rules.aspx?ID=3320) into a minor miracle. They spend 1 [Mythic Point](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323), then casts one of the following as a 3rd-rank spell (DC 24 save as appropriate): [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1447), [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451), [_heroism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555), [_protection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1641), [_resist energy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651), or [_spiritual armament_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1687). _Aerekostes_ can use their actions to [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) spells.

_Nethys Note: The rules to worship _Aerekostes_ while possessing them and further weapon advancement are presented [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=3490)._

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Aerekostes (Item)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Soldier's Syrup](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4002)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 80

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This thick syrup sweetens potions and creates a distinctive, metallic flavor associated with the festival days in Dhuraxilis. Partygoers are fond of mixing _soldier’s syrup_ with potions to extend their release.

Activating a dose of _soldier’s syrup_ requires you to combine it with another consumable potion that takes 1 action to activate. After swirling the mixture for a few moments, you must either imbibe the augmented potion within 1 minute, or the potion reverts to its unaugmented state. The potion’s level cannot exceed that of the _soldier’s syrup_ it is mixed with, otherwise the syrup is wasted.

When consumed, a _soldier’s syrup_–augmented potion does not immediately grant its benefits to you. Instead, you can activate the potion within the next hour as a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. You can have only one _soldier’s syrup_–augmented potion consumed at a time; consuming a second one renders the first one inert. If you do not activate the potion within 1 hour, the potion spoils inside you, and you become [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 24 hours.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Soldier's Syrup](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4002)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 80

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This thick syrup sweetens potions and creates a distinctive, metallic flavor associated with the festival days in Dhuraxilis. Partygoers are fond of mixing _soldier’s syrup_ with potions to extend their release.

Activating a dose of _soldier’s syrup_ requires you to combine it with another consumable potion that takes 1 action to activate. After swirling the mixture for a few moments, you must either imbibe the augmented potion within 1 minute, or the potion reverts to its unaugmented state. The potion’s level cannot exceed that of the _soldier’s syrup_ it is mixed with, otherwise the syrup is wasted.

When consumed, a _soldier’s syrup_–augmented potion does not immediately grant its benefits to you. Instead, you can activate the potion within the next hour as a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait. You can have only one _soldier’s syrup_–augmented potion consumed at a time; consuming a second one renders the first one inert. If you do not activate the potion within 1 hour, the potion spoils inside you, and you become [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 24 hours.

    pfs="Limited"
>
[Sparking Pepper String](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4003)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 80

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>[Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Vendors sell these strings of roasted peppers on festival nights in Dhuraxilis. Their spiciness cuts through the fatty meats that are also popular during late nights, and the sparks from the peppers serve as a pick-me-up to maintain the energy for a party. After you eat a sparking pepper string, your body bristles with [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) until the end of your next turn. When you successfully [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) a target with an unarmed attack or a metal weapon, you can end this effect as a free action to have the attack deal an additional 1d6 electricity damage. If not expended before the duration ends, the electricity dissipates harmlessly.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sparking Pepper String

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Hydra Spear](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4004)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 80

**Price** 465 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=361)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=361)_ of polished white bone features a tip of layered reptilian scales, compressed to form a sharp edge. Akki crafted this spear herself after she and her allies tangled with a [hydra](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=499) plaguing the marshes outside Aelyosos. She pressed the scales of the creature down into a spearpoint mostly through sheer stubbornness, granting it a unique ability.

**Activate—Hydra Swipe** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The spearhead unravels and elongates into a group of snake heads. Make up to three melee [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the _Hydra Spear_, each at a different creature within 20 feet. These attacks do not count toward your multiple attack penalty until all attacks have been resolved.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hydra Spear

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Mask of Shifting Monstrosity](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4005)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane
](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 80

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This mask’s features shift between various nonhuman elements, with each version reveling in grotesque and monstrous variations. During the festivals in Dhuraxilis, these masks are worn by influential and rich humans (or human-appearing populations) to better blend in with the monstrous crowds as they join in the revelry. While wearing the mask, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks.

**Activate—Monstrosity Meld** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You take on the general appearance of a type of non-humanoid creature that you can see, with the transformation lasting for 8 hours. Activating this effect doesn’t change your traits or statistics, nor does it grant any of the special abilities of the creature you’re imitating. It does not change your size, so a Medium creature that assumes the shape of a manticore remains Medium sized. This effect uses the same rules as the [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) activity of [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38). Onlookers always assume you’re the chosen type of creature unless they’re actively Seeking. You gain a +4 item bonus to your Deception DC against such Perception checks and add your level even if untrained.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mask of Shifting Monstrosity

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker **Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane ](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 80 **Price** 700 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- The _Staff of the Ruling Beast_ appears as though it’s made of myriad organic materials crudely woven together, from sinew and hide to bone and scales. The staff serves as a ceremonial sign of Iapholi’s authority, and on rare occasions, she loans it to favored allies to speak in her name. Even then, she expects the staff will be returned at the task’s conclusion, sending ferocious bounty hunters to retrieve it, if necessary. **Activate—Overwhelming Sound** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** With three sharp taps on the ground, the _Staff of the Ruling Beast_ casts [_bullhorn_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1971) as a 5th-rank cantrip, affecting you so long as you hold the staff. While affected, you can make your words rumble with palpable force, allowing creatures (including those who cannot hear) to feel your speech and identify your location as if they were using an imprecise sense. **Activate** [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list.
  • Cantrip [_concordant choir_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1979)
  • 1st [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [_ventriloquism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740)
  • 2nd [_blistering invective_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1970), [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458)
  • 3rd [_dream message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1503), [_enthrall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516)
**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed ranks of all listed spells.
name: Staff of the Ruling Beast

](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 81

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This strand of charms depicts the legendary [harpy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3046) Ekriathe and her six reincarnated successors. Each charm can be removed and activated. When all of the charms are consumed, the strand becomes inert.

**Activate—Hero-God's Whisper** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640))    **Effect** You detach a charm, which creates a glowing ghostly image of the hero-god depicted on it. This image sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 20-foot radius (and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for the next 20 feet) for 1 hour. Allies within the image’s bright light hear the constant whispers of Ekriathe or her successor’s voice, and they gain a +2 status bonus to saving throws against [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) effects. Enemies entering or ending their term in the image’s radius of bright light must attempt a DC 23 Will save. On a failure, the target is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) and takes a –2 status penalty to auditory-based [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) checks while they remain in the radius. Creatures that succeed against this effect are temporarily immune for the next 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Strand of the Seven Births

May contain spoilers from Myth-Speaker **Source** [Pathfinder #218: Titanbane ](/Sources.aspx?ID=274) pg. 81 **Price** 150 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- This reversible doll shows Iapholi on one side and the [harpy](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3046) Ekriathe who preceded her in her line of reincarnation on the other. Non-magical successor dolls are popular gifts for children, and Iapholi often distributes them (including a handful of magical versions) on her birthday. Magical _successor dolls_ sometimes frown, smile, or wink as if aware of their surroundings and nearby conversations. **Activate—Big Eyes, Pleading Face** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail or critically fail to [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) a favor; **Effect** The doll’s eyes grow large as its lips quiver, expressing adorable disappointment. Roll 1d4 and add the result to your [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) check as a circumstance bonus, which might improve the degree of success. **Activate—Fierce Gaze, Approving Smile** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You succeed or critically succeed when [Coercing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) a creature; **Effect** The doll smugly smirks at the creature, either reinforcing how wise it is to heed your demands or innocently reminding it how much happier it is for complying. After the creature finishes following your directives, it must attempt a DC 21 Will save. **Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature’s fond memory of the doll’s approval overwhelms any hard feelings the creature has toward you; it doesn’t act against you as a result of your coercion unless your demands were truly shocking (such as forcing them to violate any anathema they have or betray a close ally).
name: Successor Doll

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Companion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=29" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 222

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) This item uses the same defense stats as [light barding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2778)._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn barding

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Consisting of long steel spikes projecting from light barding, _anti-dragon barding_ helps to protect vulnerable mounts and livestock from massive aerial predators, including dragons, griffons, and rocs. An [animal companion](/Rules.aspx?ID=2113) wearing anti-dragon barding gains a +2 item bonus to its Fortitude DC and Reflex DC against attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376), [Reposition](/Actions.aspx?ID=2379), [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380), or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) it, and a creature that successfully Grapples the animal companion takes 1d4 piercing damage.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragon Pearl](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4011)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 222

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The damage type(s) for this item must be chosen at purchase or when crafted._

**Price** 9,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When an irritant is caught within a dragon’s scales, the dragon’s body protects itself by coating the object in layers of dragon scale. Over time, it can form an item called a _dragon pearl_. Such pearls, hard and iridescent, are infused with draconic magic and can be powerful tools in the right hands. They’re usually placed in an ornate setting on a pin or necklace. Wearing the _dragon pearl_ grants you a +2 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks. 

**Activate—Pearlescent Shell** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You call protective power from the pearl to form a defensive barrier around yourself. You’re affected by an 8th-rank [_containment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1981) spell, which takes on the appearance of shimmering translucent dragon scales. The field has [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 15 to one damage type related to the dragon the pearl came from. When you activate the pearl, you choose the type from all types the dragon has immunity or resistance to plus a type based on their tradition: [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610) for [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) for [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) for [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) for [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragon Pearl

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragon's Tongue](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4012)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 222

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The damage type(s) for this item must be chosen at purchase or when crafted._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=361)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+2 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=361)_ was crafted by a renowned monster hunter, who designed the prototype from the bones and scales of a [diabolic dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2939). The drathyrican folds of a young dragon, the organ that generates their magical breath, are crucial to the operation of the spear. The mechanics of the weapon are largely based on an [injection spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=170): when the safety is disengaged and a secondary lever is squeezed, it produces a magical effect like a dragon’s breath from the hollow tip of the spear. 

**Activate—Breath of the Spear** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** The dragon’s tongue releases a burst of energy that manifests as a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) dealing 6d6 damage with a DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. You can’t use this activation again for 1d4 rounds. The damage type matches the breath of the dragon the weapon was made from, and this activation has the corresponding trait.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the drathyrican folds of a [dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=395).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

This _[+2 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [longspear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=361)_ was crafted by a renowned monster hunter, who designed the prototype from the bones and scales of a [diabolic dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2939). The drathyrican folds of a young dragon, the organ that generates their magical breath, are crucial to the operation of the spear. The mechanics of the weapon are largely based on an [injection spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=170): when the safety is disengaged and a secondary lever is squeezed, it produces a magical effect like a dragon’s breath from the hollow tip of the spear. 

**Activate—Breath of the Spear** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** The dragon’s tongue releases a burst of energy that manifests as a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) dealing 6d6 damage with a DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. You can’t use this activation again for 1d4 rounds. The damage type matches the breath of the dragon the weapon was made from, and this activation has the corresponding trait.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the drathyrican folds of a [dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=395).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragonaut's Wingsuit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4013)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 223

**Price** 625 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+1 resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)_ is made from [drake](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=404) hide, and includes wing-like membranes that connect the limbs to one another. Originally designed to allow riders of aerial mounts to safely dismount if the need arises, such wingsuits remain uncommon but have gained some popularity among thrill-seeking adventurers and some mountain-dwelling communities.

**Activate—Drake's Glide** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You take 2 [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) actions, each of which can be a [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305). If you don’t have a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350), you gain a 20-foot fly Speed for Fly actions from this activation.

**Activate—Descend** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Trigger** You're falling;  **Effect** Your fall slows to 60 feet per round for 1 minute, and if you reach the ground during this time you take no damage from the fall.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragonaut's Wingsuit

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragon Eye](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4014)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 223

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>Cast a Spell (add 1 action)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Even in death, a dragon’s gaze is terrible to behold. When used to cast [_breathe fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457) at 3rd rank or higher, an eye from a dragon causes the spell to manifest some of the creature’s fearsome aura and inspire terror. Creatures in the spell’s area must attempt a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save instead of a basic Reflex save, and in addition to taking the spell’s normal damage on a failure, they’re [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1. This adds the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) traits to the spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragon Eye

[Dragon Scute](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4015)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Catalyst" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=396" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 223

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The thick plates that form the strongest points in a dragon’s armor can be used as powerful defensive catalysts. Using one of these scales to cast [_mountain resilience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1610) causes the target’s skin to grow a thick layer of draconic scales. Instead of the spell’s normal effects, the target gains damage [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) based on the type and dragon’s tradition.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Lesser Dragon Scute
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 223

**Price** 400 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The scale can be used with _mountain resilience_ of 6th rank or higher.

**[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1)** resistance 5 against all damage from spells 

[**Divine**](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) resistance 10 to [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), and [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 

**[Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)** resistance 10 to mental 

**[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)** resistance 7 to bludgeoning, piercing, slashing, and [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)

right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The scale can be used with _mountain resilience_ of 8th rank or higher.

**[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1)** [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 7 against all damage from spells 

**[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)** resistance 15 to [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), and [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Pact of the Herald and Host](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4016)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Contract" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=329" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 226

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You have exchanged your voice for the authority and breath of your bound dragon. Your voice echoes with the dragon’s idiosyncrasies. You can cast [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) as an [innate spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=2232) once per day, with the spell’s tradition matching that of your bound dragon. 

Once per day, from any distance, your bound dragon can choose to take control of your voice. They dictate everything you say (or don’t say) for up to 10 minutes. They can choose to speak in any language you or they know, though they can’t use your voice to cast spells or activate items.

**Activate—Breath of the Dragon** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You exhale a torrent of energy in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) or a 60-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 1d6 damage per level. Each creature in the area must attempt a basic saving throw against the higher of your class DC or spell DC. You can’t use this ability again for 1d4 rounds. The area, damage type, and type of saving throw match those of your draconic benefactor. This ability has the trait associated with the type of damage it deals.  

---

**Oathbreaker’s Calamity** Should either party break the oath, your throat and mouth become swollen and elongated, preventing you from speaking any language but [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) and causing any use of the Dragon Breath ability granted by this pact to also damage you. The dragon becomes [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66), but call still hear your voice.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pact of the Herald and Host

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Duty of the Oathsworn](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4017)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Contract" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=329" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 227

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The oath that ties you to your bound dragon transcends the boundaries of life and death. When the pact is made, the dragon chooses one type of creature other than humanoid. (The GM selects the appropriate trait.) You gain resistance equal to half your level to physical and mental damage from these creatures. You also gain the Draconic Precision action. If you die from damage delivered by a creature of this type, you rise as an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature of the dragon’s choice. This undead’s level can be no higher than your level or your bound dragon’s, whichever is lower. This undead creature has your mind; if it’s normally [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652), it loses that trait and any immunity to [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647). 

As you are sworn to slay the enemies of the dragon, the dragon is sworn to protect a chosen group, person, or location chosen by you. When battling in defense of your chosen ward, the dragon bypasses 5 [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) of any creature they would damage.

**Activate—Draconic Precision** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your Strikes against the chosen type of enemy deal an additional 1d6 precision damage for 1 minute. This damage increases to 2d6 if you’re 17th level or higher.  

---

**Oathbreaker’s Calamity** Should either party break the oath, both you and the dragon are forever forsworn from comradery. Creatures can’t treat either of you as an ally while in combat.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Duty of the Oathsworn

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[The Timeless Bond](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4018)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Contract" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=329" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 227

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You’ve linked your lifespan to your bound dragon. Your natural physical aging ceases and you gain the Draconic Vitality action, but your soul grows withered and scabrous by the weight of a dragon’s experience with time.

Once per week, from any distance, the dragon can use a free action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait, to become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 minute. They can use the extra action to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), or to contribute an action to casting a spell. When the dragon uses this ability, you age 10 years and become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 3 for 1 hour.

**Activate—Draconic Vitality** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Reduce your [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) value by 2. For 1 hour, you gain a +2 status bonus to saves against [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) and [diseases](/Rules.aspx?ID=2767), and a status bonus equal to your level to Hit Points you regain from spells with the same tradition as your bound dragon.  

---

**Oathbreaker’s Calamity** Should either party break the oath, you begin aging at an accelerated rate. Every 1d6 days, you must succeed at a [flat DC 14 check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) or age 10 years and become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 3. In turn, the dragon loses years of experience, reducing its age 100 years every time you fail the flat check. If the dragon is de-aged past the time they made the pact, the pact remains, but the dragon loses all memories of you and the pact.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: The Timeless Bond

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Enkindled Quintessence](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4019)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Contract" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=329" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 227

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You have fully subsumed your soul within that of a dragon’s soul, overloading your very essence with eldritch potential. You immediately retrain your class to that of a [sorcerer](/Classes.aspx?ID=62) with the [draconic bloodline](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=23), with your draconic exemplar matching your bound dragon. You are able to [cast spells](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) immediately as if you had a full 8 hours of rest and completed your [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575); however, you become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2 from the strain of igniting your soul’s magical potential.

Once per day, from any distance, the dragon can either [_teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720) you to their lair or teleport to switch places with you. This is a 3-action activity with the teleportation trait and the dragon’s tradition trait.

---

**Oathbreaker’s Calamity** Should either party break the oath, both your souls begin to immolate through your physical forms. If either of you casts a spell, they immediately become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 3 unless they’re casting it to harm the other party. If either of you die as a result of this calamity, you rise as a [wraith](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=164) (if it’s you) or a [wyrmwraith](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=116) (if it’s the dragon).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Enkindled Quintessence

markdown:
**Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 227 --- Your very quintessence has been hijacked by the agony of [Dahak](/Deities.aspx?ID=337) that lingered within your bound dragon. The wracked god’s whispers burn your soul and ride your body to work in dire concordance with his whims. You are the very face of horror, an heir of the Dragon Plague’s rancor, the Endless Destruction made manifest in an overwhelmed mortal form. You gain the Scream of Unending Destruction activity. In exchange, the dragon who bound you to this pact is now free from the influences of Dahak, losing any boons or abilities that might have been granted if they were a follower of Dahak and gaining a +2 item bonus on saving throws against any spells or abilities cast by followers of Dahak. **Activate—Scream of Unending Destruction** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Attempt an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check against the Will DC of each creature in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) that you observe and that observes you. **Critical Success** The target must attempt a Fortitude save against your Intimidation DC. On a critical failure, it dies in an explosion of hellfire that deals 6d6 fire damage to each creature in a 5-foot emanation. On any other result, the creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 3 and is [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) for 1 round. The critical failure effect has the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) trait.
**Success** The target becomes frightened 3 and takes 4d6 fire damage.
**Failure** The target becomes frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The target is unaffected. --- **Oathbreaker’s Calamity** The souls of both parties begin to become consumed by the agony of Dahak. At the end of each day, you and your dragon become [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1 (or increase your doomed values by 1 if you’re already doomed) unless both of you acted in service to Dahak that day. If you or your bound dragon die while under this effect, both of you explode in a catastrophe of hellfire and pitch, dealing 16d6 fire damage to all creatures in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387); this effect has the [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) and [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) traits. If the creature who didn’t die also survives this blast, the pact of the agonized destroyer ends.
name: Pact of the Agonized Destroyer

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 22"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Shadewither Key](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4021)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=274" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 229

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This palm-sized talisman hangs on a string of braided bark fibers. It resembles a leaf from some unknown tree similar to oak, carved from jade and then dipped halfway into black ink that looks wet despite feeling completely dry. The gate key was originally created to open the aiudara known as the Seventh Arch. In recent years, corrupting magic has given it additional, stranger powers. 

When you wear and invest the _Shadewither Key_, shadows gather around you. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks. However, this boon comes with great risk: any [elf](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=60) who touches the _Shadewither Key_ is immediately afflicted with the [obnubilate curse](/Curses.aspx?ID=49). 

**Activate—Umbral Double** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You touch the _Shadewither Key_ and command the shadows it casts around you to peel away. The shadows form an umbral double that resembles you in every way except for its fiery red eyes and the expression of wicked delight on its face. The double occupies the same space as you and attempts to intercept any attacks aimed at you. A creature must succeed at a DC 11 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) when targeting you with an attack, spell, or other effect; on a failure, they hit the shadowy double instead of you. The effect lasts for 1 minute or until the double is hit, whichever comes first. Once the double is hit, it’s destroyed. As long as the double exists, you don’t gain the _Shadewither Key’s_ item bonus to Stealth checks.

**Destruction** The _Shadewither Key’s_ magic can be destroyed by exposing the artifact to uncontained teleportation energies. If touched to an unlinked or malfunctioning aiudara, the _Shadewither Key_ causes the gate to connect to a new counterpart. If this happens, the key’s magic leaves it, rendering the item useless (as described in the book).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Shadewither Key

May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 230 **Price** 3 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Strike --- This vial of glowing goo constantly sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius. When a bioluminescence bomb [strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) a creature or a hard surface, it shatters and releases the bioluminescent fluid’s energy in a flare of light. Each creature within 10 feet of where the bomb exploded must succeed at a DC 17 Reflex save or be marked with dye that continues to glow for 24 hours. An affected creature must also attempt a DC 17 Fortitude saving throw against the overwhelming burst of light. **Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is dazzled for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is blinded for 1 round, then dazzled for 1 round.
name: Bioluminescence Bomb

May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 230 **Usage** worn cloak **Bulk** L --- This cloak appears to be woven from a thousand living leaves, hungry for flesh and eager to defend the cloak’s wearer. **Activate—Bite Back** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You are damaged by a melee attack from an adjacent creature; **Effect** The leaves lash out at your attacker, rising up to reveal snapping jaws made of wicked thorns. The triggering creature must attempt a DC 17 Reflex saving throw. **Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature takes 1d4 piercing damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 2d4 piercing damage and 1 [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) damage. Cloak of Gnawing Leaves **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 230 **Price** 60 gp Cloak of Thirsty Fronds **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 230 **Price** 330 gp --- The save DC is 23, and the cloak can be activated once per minute. The leaves deal 1d6 piercing damage to a creature on a failed save, or 2d6 piercing damage and 2 bleed damage on a critical failure. Cloak of Devouring Thorns **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 230 **Price** 2,000 gp --- The save DC is 30, and the cloak can be activated once per round. The leaves deal 2d6 piercing damage to a creature on a failed save, or 4d6 piercing damage and 3 bleed damage on a critical failure.
name: Cloak of Gnawing Leaves
